# Proverbs 29

<!-- BEGIN AUTO-INDEX (verse table + nav; auto-generated — safe to regenerate) -->

↑ [Proverbs](../README.md)  ·  [‹ Proverbs 28](../Proverbs-28/README.md)  ·  [Proverbs 30 ›](../Proverbs-30/README.md)

📖 **Full text:** [BBE](BBE.md) · [WEB](WEB.md) · [KJV](KJV.md) · [ASV](ASV.md)

## Verses (27)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | A man hating sharp words and making his heart hard, will suddenly be broken and will not be made well again. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | When the upright have power, the people are glad; when an evil man is ruler, grief comes on the people. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | A man who is a lover of wisdom is a joy to his father: but he who goes in the company of loose women is a waster of wealth. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | A king, by right rule, makes the land safe; but one full of desires makes it a waste. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | A man who says smooth things to his neighbour is stretching out a net for his steps. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | In the steps of an evil man there is a net for him, but the upright man gets away quickly and is glad. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | The upright man gives attention to the cause of the poor: the evil-doer gives no thought to it. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | Men of pride are the cause of violent acts in a town, but by wise men wrath is turned away. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | If a wise man goes to law with a foolish man, he may be angry or laughing, but there will be no rest. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Men of blood are haters of the good man, and evil-doers go after his soul. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | A foolish man lets out all his wrath, but a wise man keeps it back quietly. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | If a ruler gives attention to false words, all his servants are evil-doers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | The poor man and his creditor come face to face: the Lord gives light to their eyes equally. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | The king who is a true judge in the cause of the poor, will be safe for ever on the seat of his power. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | The rod and sharp words give wisdom: but a child who is not guided is a cause of shame to his mother. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | When evil men are in power, wrongdoing is increased; but the upright will have pleasure when they see their downfall. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | Give your son training, and he will give you rest; he will give delight to your soul. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | Where there is no vision, the people are uncontrolled; but he who keeps the law will be happy. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | A servant will not be trained by words; for though the sense of the words is clear to him, he will not give attention. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | Have you seen a man who is quick with his tongue? There is more hope for a foolish man than for him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | If a servant is gently cared for from his early years, he will become a cause of sorrow in the end. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | An angry man is the cause of fighting, and a man given to wrath does much wrong. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | A man's pride will be the cause of his fall, but he who has a gentle spirit will get honour. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | A man who takes part with a thief has hate for his soul; he is put under oath, but says nothing. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | The fear of man is a cause of danger: but whoever puts his faith in the Lord will have a safe place on high. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | The approval of a ruler is desired by great numbers: but the decision in a man's cause comes from the Lord. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | An evil man is disgusting to the upright, and he who is upright is disgusting to evil-doers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
